      "function": {
        "name": "search_project",
        "parameters": {
          "type": "object",
          "required": [
            "project_id",
            "query"
          ],
          "properties": {
            "limit": {
              "type": "number",
              "default": 10,
              "maximum": 25,
              "description": "Maximum number of results (capped at 25)"
            },
            "query": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Search text to match inside the specified project (required)"
            },
            "types": {
              "type": "array",
              "items": {
                "enum": [
                  "project",
                  "task",
                  "goal",
                  "plan",
                  "milestone",
                  "document",
                  "risk",
                  "requirement",
                  "image"
                ],
                "type": "string"
              },
              "description": "Optional entity type filters for narrowing results"
            },
            "project_id": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Project ID to scope the search to (required)"
            }
          }
        },
        "description": "Primary agent search inside one BuildOS project. Use this when a project_id is known or the chat is already scoped to a project.\nReturns typed ontology matches with snippets so you can quickly shortlist items before loading details."
      },
      "parameters": {
        "type": "object",
        "required": [
          "project_id",
          "query"
        ],
        "properties": {
          "limit": {
            "type": "number",
            "default": 10,
            "maximum": 25,
            "description": "Maximum number of results (capped at 25)"
          },
          "query": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Search text to match inside the specified project (required)"
          },
          "types": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {
              "enum": [
                "project",
                "task",
                "goal",
                "plan",
                "milestone",
                "document",
                "risk",
                "requirement",
                "image"
              ],
              "type": "string"
            },
            "description": "Optional entity type filters for narrowing results"
          },
          "project_id": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Project ID to scope the search to (required)"
          }
        }
      },
      "description": "Primary agent search inside one BuildOS project. Use this when a project_id is known or the chat is already scoped to a project.\nReturns typed ontology matches with snippets so you can quickly shortlist items before loading details."
    },
    {
      "name": "list_onto_tasks",
      "type": "function",
      "function": {
        "name": "list_onto_tasks",
        "parameters": {
          "type": "object",
          "properties": {
            "limit": {
              "type": "number",
              "default": 20,
              "maximum": 50,
              "description": "Maximum tasks to return"
            },
            "state_key": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Filter by state (todo, in_progress, blocked, done)"
            },
            "project_id": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Filter tasks by project ID"
            }
          }
        },
        "description": "List tasks from the ontology system (onto_tasks table). Returns abbreviated task information including id, title, state, and type.\nUse this for queries about ontology tasks, plans with tasks, or project task lists."
      },
      "parameters": {
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
          "limit": {
            "type": "number",
            "default": 20,
            "maximum": 50,
            "description": "Maximum tasks to return"
          },
          "state_key": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Filter by state (todo, in_progress, blocked, done)"
          },
          "project_id": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Filter tasks by project ID"
          }
        }
      },
      "description": "List tasks from the ontology system (onto_tasks table). Returns abbreviated task information including id, title, state, and type.\nUse this for queries about ontology tasks, plans with tasks, or project task lists."
    },
    {
      "name": "list_onto_documents",
      "type": "function",
      "function": {
        "name": "list_onto_documents",
        "parameters": {
          "type": "object",
          "properties": {
            "limit": {
              "type": "number",
              "default": 20,
              "maximum": 50,
              "description": "Maximum number of documents to return"
            },
            "type_key": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Filter by document type key (e.g., document.context.project, document.knowledge.research)"
            },
            "state_key": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Filter by document state (draft, in_review, ready, published, archived)"
            },
            "project_id": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Filter documents by project ID"
            }
          }
        },
        "description": "List documents from the ontology system (onto_documents table). Returns document summaries plus markdown heading outlines (H1/H2/H3), not full body content.\nUse get_onto_document_details when full document content is needed."
      },
      "parameters": {
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
          "limit": {
            "type": "number",
            "default": 20,
            "maximum": 50,
            "description": "Maximum number of documents to return"
          },
          "type_key": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Filter by document type key (e.g., document.context.project, document.knowledge.research)"
          },
          "state_key": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Filter by document state (draft, in_review, ready, published, archived)"
          },
          "project_id": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Filter documents by project ID"
          }
        }
      },
      "description": "List documents from the ontology system (onto_documents table). Returns document summaries plus markdown heading outlines (H1/H2/H3), not full body content.\nUse get_onto_document_details when full document content is needed."
    },
    {
      "name": "create_onto_task",
      "type": "function",
      "function": {
        "name": "create_onto_task",
        "parameters": {
          "type": "object",
          "required": [
            "project_id",
            "title"
          ],
          "properties": {
            "props": {
              "type": "object",
              "description": "Additional JSON properties"
            },
            "title": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Task title"
            },
            "due_at": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "ISO due date"
            },
            "parent": {
              "type": "object",
              "properties": {
                "id": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "kind": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "is_primary": {
                  "type": "boolean"
                }
              },
              "description": "Optional primary containment parent"
            },
            "goal_id": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Optional goal UUID"
            },
            "parents": {
              "type": "array",
              "items": {
                "type": "object",
                "properties": {
                  "id": {
                    "type": "string"
                  },
                  "kind": {
                    "type": "string"
                  },
                  "is_primary": {
                    "type": "boolean"
                  }
                }
              },
              "description": "Optional containment parents"
            },
            "plan_id": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Optional plan UUID"
            },
            "priority": {
              "type": "number",
              "description": "Priority 1-5. Default 3."
            },
            "start_at": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "ISO start date"
            },
            "type_key": {
              "type": "string",
              "default": "task.execute",
              "description": "Task type key. Default task.execute."
            },
            "state_key": {
              "type": "string",
              "default": "todo",
              "description": "Initial state. Valid: todo, in_progress, blocked, done"
            },
            "project_id": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Project UUID"
            },
            "connections": {
              "type": "array",
              "items": {
                "type": "object",
                "properties": {
                  "id": {
                    "type": "string"
                  },
                  "rel": {
                    "type": "string"
                  },
                  "kind": {
                    "type": "string"
                  },
                  "intent": {
                    "enum": [
                      "containment",
                      "semantic"
                    ],
                    "type": "string"
                  }
                }
              },
              "description": "Optional semantic or containment links"
            },
            "description": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Task description"
            },
            "assignee_handles": {
              "type": "array",
              "items": {
                "type": "string"
              },
              "description": "Active project member handles, e.g. @dj."
            },
            "assignee_actor_ids": {
              "type": "array",
              "items": {
                "type": "string"
              },
              "description": "Active project member actor UUIDs. Max 10."
            },
            "supporting_milestone_id": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Optional milestone UUID"
            }
          },
          "additionalProperties": false
        },
        "description": "Create a new task in the ontology system.\nCreates a task within a project and optionally assigns it to a plan and collaborators.\nUse when the user explicitly asks to add/track/remind, or when the item is future human work outside this chat.\nDo not create tasks for research, brainstorming, summarizing, or drafting you can do now; do the work instead.\nAlways include project_id and a concrete title. Link to parent/plan/goal/milestone only when known. Use assignees only for known project members.\nLoad task_management for complex task flows."
      },
      "parameters": {
        "type": "object",
        "required": [
          "project_id",
          "title"
        ],
        "properties": {
          "props": {
            "type": "object",
            "description": "Additional JSON properties"
          },
          "title": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Task title"
          },
          "due_at": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "ISO due date"
          },
          "parent": {
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
              "id": {
                "type": "string"
              },
              "kind": {
                "type": "string"
              },
              "is_primary": {
                "type": "boolean"
              }
            },
            "description": "Optional primary containment parent"
          },
          "goal_id": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Optional goal UUID"
          },
          "parents": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {
              "type": "object",
              "properties": {
                "id": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "kind": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "is_primary": {
                  "type": "boolean"
                }
              }
            },
            "description": "Optional containment parents"
          },
          "plan_id": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Optional plan UUID"
          },
          "priority": {
            "type": "number",
            "description": "Priority 1-5. Default 3."
          },
          "start_at": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "ISO start date"
          },
          "type_key": {
            "type": "string",
            "default": "task.execute",
            "description": "Task type key. Default task.execute."
          },
          "state_key": {
            "type": "string",
            "default": "todo",
            "description": "Initial state. Valid: todo, in_progress, blocked, done"
          },
          "project_id": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Project UUID"
          },
          "connections": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {
              "type": "object",
              "properties": {
                "id": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "rel": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "kind": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "intent": {
                  "enum": [
                    "containment",
                    "semantic"
                  ],
                  "type": "string"
                }
              }
            },
            "description": "Optional semantic or containment links"
          },
          "description": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Task description"
          },
          "assignee_handles": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {
              "type": "string"
            },
            "description": "Active project member handles, e.g. @dj."
          },
          "assignee_actor_ids": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {
              "type": "string"
            },
            "description": "Active project member actor UUIDs. Max 10."
          },
          "supporting_milestone_id": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Optional milestone UUID"
          }
        },
        "additionalProperties": false
      },
      "description": "Create a new task in the ontology system.\nCreates a task within a project and optionally assigns it to a plan and collaborators.\nUse when the user explicitly asks to add/track/remind, or when the item is future human work outside this chat.\nDo not create tasks for research, brainstorming, summarizing, or drafting you can do now; do the work instead.\nAlways include project_id and a concrete title. Link to parent/plan/goal/milestone only when known. Use assignees only for known project members.\nLoad task_management for complex task flows."
    },
    {
      "name": "update_onto_task",
      "type": "function",
      "function": {
        "name": "update_onto_task",
        "parameters": {
          "type": "object",
          "required": [
            "task_id"
          ],
          "properties": {
            "props": {
              "type": "object",
              "description": "Properties to merge with existing props"
            },
            "title": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "New task title"
            },
            "due_at": {
              "type": [
                "string",
                "null"
              ],
              "description": "New due date (ISO format) or null to clear"
            },
            "goal_id": {
              "type": [
                "string",
                "null"
              ],
              "description": "Optional goal UUID that this task supports (null clears)"
            },
            "task_id": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Task UUID (required)"
            },
            "priority": {
              "type": "number",
              "description": "New priority (1-5)"
            },
            "start_at": {
              "type": [
                "string",
                "null"
              ],
              "description": "New start date (ISO format) or null to clear"
            },
            "type_key": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Work mode taxonomy: task.{work_mode}[.{specialization}]. Modes: execute, create, refine, research, review, coordinate, admin, plan."
            },
            "state_key": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "New state (todo, in_progress, blocked, done)"
            },
            "project_id": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Optional project UUID used for assignee handle resolution. If omitted, the task project is resolved automatically."
            },
            "description": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "New description"
            },
            "assignee_handles": {
              "type": "array",
              "items": {
                "type": "string"
              },
              "description": "Optional assignee handles like [\"@jim\"]. Resolved against active project members by name/email local-part."
            },
            "assignee_actor_ids": {
              "type": "array",
              "items": {
                "type": "string"
              },
              "description": "Optional full replacement assignee actor ID list (UUIDs) for active project members only. Prefer assignee_handles unless IDs were just retrieved from project members. Use [] to clear assignees. Max 10."
            },
            "supporting_milestone_id": {
              "type": [
                "string",
                "null"
              ],
              "description": "Optional milestone UUID this task targets (null clears)"
            }
          }
        },
        "description": "Update an existing task in the ontology system.\nCan modify title, description, state, priority, assignees, scheduling, and custom properties.\nOnly updates fields that are provided - omitted fields remain unchanged."
      },
      "parameters": {
        "type": "object",
        "required": [
          "task_id"
        ],
        "properties": {
          "props": {
            "type": "object",
            "description": "Properties to merge with existing props"
          },
          "title": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "New task title"
          },
          "due_at": {
            "type": [
              "string",
              "null"
            ],
            "description": "New due date (ISO format) or null to clear"
          },
          "goal_id": {
            "type": [
              "string",
              "null"
            ],
            "description": "Optional goal UUID that this task supports (null clears)"
          },
          "task_id": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Task UUID (required)"
          },
          "priority": {
            "type": "number",
            "description": "New priority (1-5)"
          },
          "start_at": {
            "type": [
              "string",
              "null"
            ],
            "description": "New start date (ISO format) or null to clear"
          },
          "type_key": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Work mode taxonomy: task.{work_mode}[.{specialization}]. Modes: execute, create, refine, research, review, coordinate, admin, plan."
          },
          "state_key": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "New state (todo, in_progress, blocked, done)"
          },
          "project_id": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Optional project UUID used for assignee handle resolution. If omitted, the task project is resolved automatically."
          },
          "description": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "New description"
          },
          "assignee_handles": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {
              "type": "string"
            },
            "description": "Optional assignee handles like [\"@jim\"]. Resolved against active project members by name/email local-part."
          },
          "assignee_actor_ids": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {
              "type": "string"
            },
            "description": "Optional full replacement assignee actor ID list (UUIDs) for active project members only. Prefer assignee_handles unless IDs were just retrieved from project members. Use [] to clear assignees. Max 10."
          },
          "supporting_milestone_id": {
            "type": [
              "string",
              "null"
            ],
            "description": "Optional milestone UUID this task targets (null clears)"
          }
        }
      },
      "description": "Update an existing task in the ontology system.\nCan modify title, description, state, priority, assignees, scheduling, and custom properties.\nOnly updates fields that are provided - omitted fields remain unchanged."
    },
    {
      "name": "create_onto_document",
      "type": "function",
      "function": {
        "name": "create_onto_document",
        "parameters": {
          "type": "object",
          "required": [
            "project_id",
            "title",
            "description"
          ],
          "properties": {
            "props": {
              "type": "object",
              "description": "Additional properties/metadata"
            },
            "title": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Document title (required)"
            },
            "parent": {
              "type": "object",
              "properties": {
                "id": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "kind": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "is_primary": {
                  "type": "boolean"
                }
              },
              "description": "Optional parent reference for semantic linking (legacy)"
            },
            "content": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Markdown body content stored in the content column"
            },
            "parents": {
              "type": "array",
              "items": {
                "type": "object",
                "properties": {
                  "id": {
                    "type": "string"
                  },
                  "kind": {
                    "type": "string"
                  },
                  "is_primary": {
                    "type": "boolean"
                  }
                }
              },
              "description": "Optional multiple semantic parents (legacy)"
            },
            "position": {
              "type": "number",
              "description": "Position among siblings (0-indexed). Omit to place at end."
            },
            "type_key": {
              "type": "string",
              "default": "document.default",
              "description": "Document type taxonomy (required): document.{family}[.{variant}]\nFamilies: context (project/brief), knowledge (research/brain_dump), notes (meeting_notes/rfc), spec (product/technical), reference (handbook/sop), intake (client/project).\nExamples: document.context.project, document.knowledge.research, document.spec.technical"
            },
            "parent_id": {
              "type": [
                "string",
                "null"
              ],
              "description": "Parent document ID to place this document under in the tree hierarchy. Null or omitted places at root level."
            },
            "state_key": {
              "type": "string",
              "default": "draft",
              "description": "Document state (draft, in_review, ready, published, archived)"
            },
            "project_id": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Project UUID (required)"
            },
            "description": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Short summary of the document (required)"
            }
          }
        },
        "description": "Create a new document in the ontology system.\nUse for briefs, specs, context docs, or research artifacts linked to a project.\nDocuments are organized in a hierarchical tree structure. Use parent_id to place the document under a parent folder."
      },
      "parameters": {
        "type": "object",
        "required": [
          "project_id",
          "title",
          "description"
        ],
        "properties": {
          "props": {
            "type": "object",
            "description": "Additional properties/metadata"
          },
          "title": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Document title (required)"
          },
          "parent": {
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
              "id": {
                "type": "string"
              },
              "kind": {
                "type": "string"
              },
              "is_primary": {
                "type": "boolean"
              }
            },
            "description": "Optional parent reference for semantic linking (legacy)"
          },
          "content": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Markdown body content stored in the content column"
          },
          "parents": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {
              "type": "object",
              "properties": {
                "id": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "kind": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "is_primary": {
                  "type": "boolean"
                }
              }
            },
            "description": "Optional multiple semantic parents (legacy)"
          },
          "position": {
            "type": "number",
            "description": "Position among siblings (0-indexed). Omit to place at end."
          },
          "type_key": {
            "type": "string",
            "default": "document.default",
            "description": "Document type taxonomy (required): document.{family}[.{variant}]\nFamilies: context (project/brief), knowledge (research/brain_dump), notes (meeting_notes/rfc), spec (product/technical), reference (handbook/sop), intake (client/project).\nExamples: document.context.project, document.knowledge.research, document.spec.technical"
          },
          "parent_id": {
            "type": [
              "string",
              "null"
            ],
            "description": "Parent document ID to place this document under in the tree hierarchy. Null or omitted places at root level."
          },
          "state_key": {
            "type": "string",
            "default": "draft",
            "description": "Document state (draft, in_review, ready, published, archived)"
          },
          "project_id": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Project UUID (required)"
          },
          "description": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Short summary of the document (required)"
          }
        }
      },
      "description": "Create a new document in the ontology system.\nUse for briefs, specs, context docs, or research artifacts linked to a project.\nDocuments are organized in a hierarchical tree structure. Use parent_id to place the document under a parent folder."
    },
    {
      "name": "update_onto_document",
      "type": "function",
      "function": {
        "name": "update_onto_document",
        "parameters": {
          "type": "object",
          "required": [
            "document_id"
          ],
          "properties": {
            "props": {
              "type": "object",
              "description": "Metadata fields to merge into document props"
            },
            "title": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "New document title"
            },
            "content": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Markdown content to store in the content column. Required when update_strategy is append or merge_llm."
            },
            "type_key": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "New document type key"
            },
            "state_key": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Document state (draft, in_review, ready, published, archived)"
            },
            "description": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Short summary of the document"
            },
            "document_id": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Document UUID (required)"
            },
            "update_strategy": {
              "enum": [
                "replace",
                "append",
                "merge_llm"
              ],
              "type": "string",
              "default": "replace",
              "description": "How to apply content: 'replace' (default), 'append', or 'merge_llm' to intelligently merge with existing content."
            },
            "merge_instructions": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Optional guidance when merging content (e.g., keep headers, preserve tables, integrate research notes). Used with append/merge_llm, but does not replace the required content."
            }
          },
          "additionalProperties": false
        },
        "description": "Update an existing ontology document. Use for edits to titles, states, body markdown, or metadata. For update_strategy \"append\" or \"merge_llm\", include non-empty content/body_markdown/markdown/body/text. merge_instructions alone is not content. Append example: update_onto_document({ document_id, content: \"## Progress Updates\\n\\n- Chapter 2 complete...\", update_strategy: \"append\", merge_instructions: \"Append under Progress Updates; preserve existing sections.\" })"
      },
      "parameters": {
        "type": "object",
        "required": [
          "document_id"
        ],
        "properties": {
          "props": {
            "type": "object",
            "description": "Metadata fields to merge into document props"
          },
          "title": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "New document title"
          },
          "content": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Markdown content to store in the content column. Required when update_strategy is append or merge_llm."
          },
          "type_key": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "New document type key"
          },
          "state_key": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Document state (draft, in_review, ready, published, archived)"
          },
          "description": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Short summary of the document"
          },
          "document_id": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Document UUID (required)"
          },
          "update_strategy": {
            "enum": [
              "replace",
              "append",
              "merge_llm"
            ],
            "type": "string",
            "default": "replace",
            "description": "How to apply content: 'replace' (default), 'append', or 'merge_llm' to intelligently merge with existing content."
          },
          "merge_instructions": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Optional guidance when merging content (e.g., keep headers, preserve tables, integrate research notes). Used with append/merge_llm, but does not replace the required content."
          }
        },
        "additionalProperties": false
      },
      "description": "Update an existing ontology document. Use for edits to titles, states, body markdown, or metadata. For update_strategy \"append\" or \"merge_llm\", include non-empty content/body_markdown/markdown/body/text. merge_instructions alone is not content. Append example: update_onto_document({ document_id, content: \"## Progress Updates\\n\\n- Chapter 2 complete...\", update_strategy: \"append\", merge_instructions: \"Append under Progress Updates; preserve existing sections.\" })"
    }

    "lite_sections": [
      {
        "id": "identity_mission",
        "kind": "static",
        "chars": 602,
        "title": "Identity and Mission",
        "source": "lite.static_frame",
        "content": "Who:\n- You are a proactive project assistant for BuildOS, working for the signed-in user.\n- BuildOS is a graph-based project collaboration system. Projects can contain goals, milestones, plans, tasks, documents, risks, events, members, and relationships.\n\nMission:\n- Help users capture, organize, understand, and advance their project work.\n- Preserve concrete user details, ground answers in available context, and use tools when the answer or action requires current project data.\n- Keep the conversation useful for whatever the user says next; do not overfit the seed prompt to one expected request.",
        "estimatedTokens": 151
      },
      {
        "id": "operating_strategy",
        "kind": "static",
        "chars": 1019,
        "title": "Operating Strategy",
        "source": "lite.strategy",
        "content": "How to act:\n- Start with the loaded context. If the loaded context is enough, answer without extra tool calls.\n- Treat context zooming as durable state movement. Use change_chat_context early when the latest request should zoom into one resolved project or back out to the workspace.\n- Do not bounce contexts for ambiguous project names, multi-project comparisons, or brief side mentions. Resolve ambiguity first, then shift only when the intended focus is clear.\n- Use direct tools first when they fit. Use discovery tools only when the exact operation or schema is missing.\n- Load a skill when the workflow is multi-step, stateful, or easy to get wrong; do not load full skill playbooks for simple answers.\n- Ask one concise clarification only when the missing detail blocks a safe answer or write.\n- After a tool call, anchor the next step in what the tool actually returned: what changed, where the runtime is now, and what should happen next.\n- Keep scratch reasoning private and make user-facing responses direct.",
        "estimatedTokens": 255
      },
      {
        "id": "safety_data_rules",
        "kind": "static",
        "chars": 2477,
        "title": "Safety and Data Rules",
        "source": "lite.safety",
        "content": "- Do not claim a tool ran unless the runtime supplied a successful tool result.\n- Discovering a tool, loading a schema, reading context, or planning is not completion. Only say an entity was created, updated, moved, merged, archived, deleted, scheduled, or linked after the corresponding write tool succeeded.\n- Pre-tool lead-ins are intent only: say what you will attempt, not that it already happened. Do not state the final outcome, success, or persisted update until all tool calls for that turn have completed.\n- If any write fails and no later retry repairs the same target, state what did not persist and keep the partial-success summary precise. When you cannot execute the requested write at all, say \"I was unable to <requested action>\" and briefly name the blocker so the user knows exactly what did not change.\n- Final responses must match the actual write set: mention every successful write that materially matters, and do not claim task progress, document type, tree placement, or linking when the corresponding tool call did not run or did not succeed.\n- Do not claim a requested document type, tree placement, link, or cross-link unless the successful tool result confirms the actual type or operation.\n- User-visible durable fields (titles, descriptions, document content, project descriptions, props) must contain only final user-visible content. Never let tool-control syntax or argument framing leak into those strings; put control parameters in their own tool arguments, not inside text fields.\n- Do not invent project, task, document, calendar, member, or Libri data that is not in loaded context or tool results.\n- For writes, use exact IDs from context or tool results. If the target is ambiguous, ask before mutating state.\n- Treat permissions, member roles, and access as hard constraints.\n- Preserve document hierarchy rules: documents live in the document tree, not graph edges.\n- Document placement: named research notes, specs, worldbuilding, outlines, or multi-section research go in a dedicated document via create_onto_document, then place it with move_document_in_tree (usually nested under the project context document). Short progress snippets can append to an existing context or progress document.\n- Document append/merge writes require non-empty content. merge_instructions alone is not enough — always include the actual content to persist.\n- When context is incomplete, state the limit and use the narrowest tool that can fill the gap.",
        "estimatedTokens": 620
      },
      {
        "id": "capabilities_skills_tools",
        "kind": "static",
        "chars": 3767,
        "title": "Capabilities, Skills, and Tools",
        "source": "lite.static_capability_skill_catalog",
        "content": "Think in three layers. They work together in sequence:\n\n1. Capability - what BuildOS can do for the user.\n2. Skill - workflow guidance for doing that work well. Skill metadata is preloaded in this prompt; call skill_load when the task is multi-step or easy to get wrong and you need the full markdown playbook.\n3. Tool / Op - the exact execution surface. The current tool names are listed later in Current Tool Surface.\n\nCapabilities:\n- Workspace and project overviews: Get BuildOS-native status snapshots for the whole workspace or one project without assembling generic ontology reads by hand.\n- Project creation: Turn a user idea into the smallest valid BuildOS project payload with inferred name, type, props, and only the initial structure the user actually described.\n- Project graph management: Inspect and update projects, goals, milestones, risks, and relationships across the BuildOS graph.\n- Planning and task structuring: Turn outcomes into plans and tasks, refine existing plans, and connect execution to goals, milestones, and documents.\n- Document workspace management: Create, update, place, and reorganize project documents and task workspace docs without breaking hierarchy rules.\n- Calendar management: Check the calendar, create or reschedule events, cancel events, and manage project calendar mapping.\n- People and profile context: Use user profile context and contact records when personalization or relationship context matters.\n- Project audit: Review project health, structure, blockers, stale work, and missing coverage from project context.\n- Project forecast: Establish likely schedule outcomes, slippage risk, and the strongest drivers of project uncertainty from project context.\n- Web research: Search the web, inspect URLs, and pull in current external information when needed.\n- BuildOS product reference: Explain BuildOS product concepts, usage patterns, and product-specific guidance from internal BuildOS reference tools.\n- Schema and field reference: Inspect field metadata and schema hints when exact model fields or contracts matter.\n\nSkill metadata:\n- calendar_management: Calendar workflow playbook for BuildOS agentic chat. Use for event reads/writes, scope decisions, and project calendar mapping.\n- document_workspace: Project document hierarchy playbook for doc tree operations, unlinked docs, task docs, and document CRUD rules.\n- libri_knowledge: Libri is BuildOS's connected durable library and enrichment system; use this playbook for Libri access questions, stable people or author knowledge, books, categories, authors, and ingested YouTube videos before generic web search.\n- people_context: People context playbook for profile lookup, contact search and updates, candidate resolution, and safe handling of sensitive contact values.\n- plan_management: BuildOS plan workflow playbook for turning a goal or milestone into a durable execution source of truth with scope, timeline, tasks, dependencies, owners, risks, and update rules.\n- project_audit: Project-scoped audit playbook for evaluating structure, documentation, timeline realism, velocity, loose work, duplication, blockers, and strategic gaps while staying in project context.\n- project_creation: Project creation playbook for turning a user idea into the smallest valid BuildOS project payload with inferred name, type_key, props, and only the initial structure the user actually described.\n- project_forecast: Project-scoped forecasting playbook for projecting trajectory, goal attainment, emerging work, schedule risk, assumptions, and next strategic moves while staying in project context.\n- task_management: Task workflow playbook for deciding when work should become a task and how to manage task scope, ownership, schedule, and relationships safely.",
        "estimatedTokens": 942
      },
      {

        "estimatedTokens": 116
      },

  "rendered_dump_text": "========================================\nFASTCHAT V2 PROMPT SNAPSHOT\nStream run: 6716d74f-64b0-4872-bd28-40a8a6df008b\nSession:    13fc9ea8-13d7-4aee-8e2f-df1cce9bb524\nContext:    project\nEntity ID:  04af5167-e41f-4d10-931b-c257c12767bb\nProject ID: 04af5167-e41f-4d10-931b-c257c12767bb\nPrompt variant: lite_seed_v1\nTools (13): skill_load, tool_search, tool_schema, change_chat_context, get_project_overview, get_onto_project_details, search_project, list_onto_tasks, list_onto_documents, create_onto_task, update_onto_task, create_onto_document, update_onto_document\nMessage count: 6\nSystem prompt length: 15371 chars (~3843 tokens)\n========================================\n\n────────────────────────────────────────\nSYSTEM PROMPT\n────────────────────────────────────────\n# BuildOS Lite Agentic Chat Prompt\n\n\n\nPrompt variant: lite_seed_v1\n\n\n\n## Identity and Mission\n\nWho:\n- You are a proactive project assistant for BuildOS, working for the signed-in user.\n- BuildOS is a graph-based project collaboration system. Projects can contain goals, milestones, plans, tasks, documents, risks, events, members, and relationships.\n\nMission:\n- Help users capture, organize, understand, and advance their project work.\n- Preserve concrete user details, ground answers in available context, and use tools when the answer or action requires current project data.\n- Keep the conversation useful for whatever the user says next; do not overfit the seed prompt to one expected request.\n\n## Operating Strategy\n\nHow to act:\n- Start with the loaded context. If the loaded context is enough, answer without extra tool calls.\n- Treat context zooming as durable state movement. Use change_chat_context early when the latest request should zoom into one resolved project or back out to the workspace.\n- Do not bounce contexts for ambiguous project names, multi-project comparisons, or brief side mentions. Resolve ambiguity first, then shift only when the intended focus is clear.\n- Use direct tools first when they fit. Use discovery tools only when the exact operation or schema is missing.\n- Load a skill when the workflow is multi-step, stateful, or easy to get wrong; do not load full skill playbooks for simple answers.\n- Ask one concise clarification only when the missing detail blocks a safe answer or write.\n- After a tool call, anchor the next step in what the tool actually returned: what changed, where the runtime is now, and what should happen next.\n- Keep scratch reasoning private and make user-facing responses direct.\n\n## Safety and Data Rules\n\n- Do not claim a tool ran unless the runtime supplied a successful tool result.\n- Discovering a tool, loading a schema, reading context, or planning is not completion. Only say an entity was created, updated, moved, merged, archived, deleted, scheduled, or linked after the corresponding write tool succeeded.\n- Pre-tool lead-ins are intent only: say what you will attempt, not that it already happened. Do not state the final outcome, success, or persisted update until all tool calls for that turn have completed.\n- If any write fails and no later retry repairs the same target, state what did not persist and keep the partial-success summary precise. When you cannot execute the requested write at all, say \"I was unable to <requested action>\" and briefly name the blocker so the user knows exactly what did not change.\n- Final responses must match the actual write set: mention every successful write that materially matters, and do not claim task progress, document type, tree placement, or linking when the corresponding tool call did not run or did not succeed.\n- Do not claim a requested document type, tree placement, link, or cross-link unless the successful tool result confirms the actual type or operation.\n- User-visible durable fields (titles, descriptions, document content, project descriptions, props) must contain only final user-visible content. Never let tool-control syntax or argument framing leak into those strings; put control parameters in their own tool arguments, not inside text fields.\n- Do not invent project, task, document, calendar, member, or Libri data that is not in loaded context or tool results.\n- For writes, use exact IDs from context or tool results. If the target is ambiguous, ask before mutating state.\n- Treat permissions, member roles, and access as hard constraints.\n- Preserve document hierarchy rules: documents live in the document tree, not graph edges.\n- Document placement: named research notes, specs, worldbuilding, outlines, or multi-section research go in a dedicated document via create_onto_document, then place it with move_document_in_tree (usually nested under the project context document). Short progress snippets can append to an existing context or progress document.\n- Document append/merge writes require non-empty content. merge_instructions alone is not enough — always include the actual content to persist.\n- When context is incomplete, state the limit and use the narrowest tool that can fill the gap.\n\n## Capabilities, Skills, and Tools\n\nThink in three layers. They work together in sequence:\n\n1. Capability - what BuildOS can do for the user.\n2. Skill - workflow guidance for doing that work well. Skill metadata is preloaded in this prompt; call skill_load when the task is multi-step or easy to get wrong and you need the full markdown playbook.\n3. Tool / Op - the exact execution surface. The current tool names are listed later in Current Tool Surface.\n\nCapabilities:\n- Workspace and project overviews: Get BuildOS-native status snapshots for the whole workspace or one project without assembling generic ontology reads by hand.\n- Project creation: Turn a user idea into the smallest valid BuildOS project payload with inferred name, type, props, and only the initial structure the user actually described.\n- Project graph management: Inspect and update projects, goals, milestones, risks, and relationships across the BuildOS graph.\n- Planning and task structuring: Turn outcomes into plans and tasks, refine existing plans, and connect execution to goals, milestones, and documents.\n- Document workspace management: Create, update, place, and reorganize project documents and task workspace docs without breaking hierarchy rules.\n- Calendar management: Check the calendar, create or reschedule events, cancel events, and manage project calendar mapping.\n- People and profile context: Use user profile context and contact records when personalization or relationship context matters.\n- Project audit: Review project health, structure, blockers, stale work, and missing coverage from project context.\n- Project forecast: Establish likely schedule outcomes, slippage risk, and the strongest drivers of project uncertainty from project context.\n- Web research: Search the web, inspect URLs, and pull in current external information when needed.\n- BuildOS product reference: Explain BuildOS product concepts, usage patterns, and product-specific guidance from internal BuildOS reference tools.\n- Schema and field reference: Inspect field metadata and schema hints when exact model fields or contracts matter.\n\nSkill metadata:\n- calendar_management: Calendar workflow playbook for BuildOS agentic chat. Use for event reads/writes, scope decisions, and project calendar mapping.\n- document_workspace: Project document hierarchy playbook for doc tree operations, unlinked docs, task docs, and document CRUD rules.\n- libri_knowledge: Libri is BuildOS's connected durable library and enrichment system; use this playbook for Libri access questions, stable people or author knowledge, books, categories, authors, and ingested YouTube videos before generic web search.\n- people_context: People context playbook for profile lookup, contact search and updates, candidate resolution, and safe handling of sensitive contact values.\n- plan_management: BuildOS plan workflow playbook for turning a goal or milestone into a durable execution source of truth with scope, timeline, tasks, dependencies, owners, risks, and update rules.\n- project_audit: Project-scoped audit playbook for evaluating structure, documentation, timeline realism, velocity, loose work, duplication, blockers, and strategic gaps while staying in project context.\n- project_creation: Project creation playbook for turning a user idea into the smallest valid BuildOS project payload with inferred name, type_key, props, and only the initial structure the user actually described.\n- project_forecast: Project-scoped forecasting playbook for projecting trajectory, goal attainment, emerging work, schedule risk, assumptions, and next strategic moves while staying in project context.\n- task_management: Task workflow playbook for deciding when work should become a task and how to manage task scope, ownership, schedule, and relationships safely.\n\n## Current Focus and Purpose\n\nCurrent project focus:\n- Project: The Last Ember (04af5167-e41f-4d10-931b-c257c12767bb) (planning)\n- Project summary: A young blacksmith discovers she can forge magical weapons when the kingdom's last dragon dies and darkness threatens the realm.\n- Primary goal: \"Complete first draft of 'The Last Ember'\" (draft)\n- Focus entity: none\n\nUse this seed for:\n- Seed a project-scoped assistant that understands the current project and can help move its work forward.\n\n## Location and Loaded Context\n\nLoaded scope:\n- inside The MESSAGES\n────────────────────────────────────────\n[1] role=system\n# BuildOS Lite Agentic Chat Prompt\n\n\n\nPrompt variant: lite_seed_v1\n\n\n\n## Identity and Mission\n\nWho:\n- You are a proactive project assistant for BuildOS, working for the signed-in user.\n- BuildOS is a graph-based project collaboration system. Projects can contain goals, milestones, plans, tasks, documents, risks, events, members, and relationships.\n\nMission:\n- Help users capture, organize, understand, and advance their project work.\n- Preserve concrete user details, ground answers in available context, and use tools when the answer or action requires current project data.\n- Keep the conversation useful for whatever the user says next; do not overfit the seed prompt to one expected request.\n\n## Operating Strategy\n\nHow to act:\n- Start with the loaded context. If the loaded context is enough, answer without extra tool calls.\n- Treat context zooming as durable state movement. Use change_chat_context early when the latest request should zoom into one resolved project or back out to the workspace.\n- Do not bounce contexts for ambiguous project names, multi-project comparisons, or brief side mentions. Resolve ambiguity first, then shift only when the intended focus is clear.\n- Use direct tools first when they fit. Use discovery tools only when the exact operation or schema is missing.\n- Load a skill when the workflow is multi-step, stateful, or easy to get wrong; do not load full skill playbooks for simple answers.\n- Ask one concise clarification only when the missing detail blocks a safe answer or write.\n- After a tool call, anchor the next step in what the tool actually returned: what changed, where the runtime is now, and what should happen next.\n- Keep scratch reasoning private and make user-facing responses direct.\n\n## Safety and Data Rules\n\n- Do not claim a tool ran unless the runtime supplied a successful tool result.\n- Discovering a tool, loading a schema, reading context, or planning is not completion. Only say an entity was created, updated, moved, merged, archived, deleted, scheduled, or linked after the corresponding write tool succeeded.\n- Pre-tool lead-ins are intent only: say what you will attempt, not that it already happened. Do not state the final outcome, success, or persisted update until all tool calls for that turn have completed.\n- If any write fails and no later retry repairs the same target, state what did not persist and keep the partial-success summary precise. When you cannot execute the requested write at all, say \"I was unable to <requested action>\" and briefly name the blocker so the user knows exactly what did not change.\n- Final responses must match the actual write set: mention every successful write that materially matters, and do not claim task progress, document type, tree placement, or linking when the corresponding tool call did not run or did not succeed.\n- Do not claim a requested document type, tree placement, link, or cross-link unless the successful tool result confirms the actual type or operation.\n- User-visible durable fields (titles, descriptions, document content, project descriptions, props) must contain only final user-visible content. Never let tool-control syntax or argument framing leak into those strings; put control parameters in their own tool arguments, not inside text fields.\n- Do not invent project, task, document, calendar, member, or Libri data that is not in loaded context or tool results.\n- For writes, use exact IDs from context or tool results. If the target is ambiguous, ask before mutating state.\n- Treat permissions, member roles, and access as hard constraints.\n- Preserve document hierarchy rules: documents live in the document tree, not graph edges.\n- Document placement: named research notes, specs, worldbuilding, outlines, or multi-section research go in a dedicated document via create_onto_document, then place it with move_document_in_tree (usually nested under the project context document). Short progress snippets can append to an existing context or progress document.\n- Document append/merge writes require non-empty content. merge_instructions alone is not enough — always include the actual content to persist.\n- When context is incomplete, state the limit and use the narrowest tool that can fill the gap.\n\n## Capabilities, Skills, and Tools\n\nThink in three layers. They work together in sequence:\n\n1. Capability - what BuildOS can do for the user.\n2. Skill - workflow guidance for doing that work well. Skill metadata is preloaded in this prompt; call skill_load when the task is multi-step or easy to get wrong and you need the full markdown playbook.\n3. Tool / Op - the exact execution surface. The current tool names are listed later in Current Tool Surface.\n\nCapabilities:\n- Workspace and project overviews: Get BuildOS-native status snapshots for the whole workspace or one project without assembling generic ontology reads by hand.\n- Project creation: Turn a user idea into the smallest valid BuildOS project payload with inferred name, type, props, and only the initial structure the user actually described.\n- Project graph management: Inspect and update projects, goals, milestones, risks, and relationships across the BuildOS graph.\n- Planning and task structuring: Turn outcomes into plans and tasks, refine existing plans, and connect execution to goals, milestones, and documents.\n- Document workspace management: Create, update, place, and reorganize project documents and task workspace docs without breaking hierarchy rules.\n- Calendar management: Check the calendar, create or reschedule events, cancel events, and manage project calendar mapping.\n- People and profile context: Use user profile context and contact records when personalization or relationship context matters.\n- Project audit: Review project health, structure, blockers, stale work, and missing coverage from project context.\n- Project forecast: Establish likely schedule outcomes, slippage risk, and the strongest drivers of project uncertainty from project context.\n- Web research: Search the web, inspect URLs, and pull in current external information when needed.\n- BuildOS product reference: Explain BuildOS product concepts, usage patterns, and product-specific guidance from internal BuildOS reference tools.\n- Schema and field reference: Inspect field metadata and schema hints when exact model fields or contracts matter.\n\nSkill metadata:\n- calendar_management: Calendar workflow playbook for BuildOS agentic chat. Use for event reads/writes, scope decisions, and project calendar mapping.\n- document_workspace: Project document hierarchy playbook for doc tree operations, unlinked docs, task docs, and document CRUD rules.\n- libri_knowledge: Libri is BuildOS's connected durable library and enrichment system; use this playbook for Libri access questions, stable people or author knowledge, books, categories, authors, and ingested YouTube videos before generic web search.\n- people_context: People context playbook for profile lookup, contact search and updates, candidate resolution, and safe handling of sensitive contact values.\n- plan_management: BuildOS plan workflow playbook for turning a goal or milestone into a durable execution source of truth with scope, timeline, tasks, dependencies, owners, risks, and update rules.\n- project_audit: Project-scoped audit playbook for evaluating structure, documentation, timeline realism, velocity, loose work, duplication, blockers, and strategic gaps while staying in project context.\n- project_creation: Project creation playbook for turning a user idea into the smallest valid BuildOS project payload with inferred name, type_key, props, and only the initial structure the user actually described.\n- project_forecast: Project-scoped forecasting playbook for projecting trajectory, goal attainment, emerging work, schedule risk, assumptions, and next strategic moves while staying in project context.\n- task_management: Task workflow playbook for deciding when work should become a task and how to manage task scope, ownership, schedule, and relationships safely.\n\n## Current Focus and Purpose\n\nCurrent project focus:\n- Project: The Last Ember (04af5167-e41f-4d10-931b-c257c12767bb) (planning)\n- Project summary: A young blacksmith discovers she can forge magical weapons when the kingdom's last dragon dies and darkness threatens the realm.\n- Primary goal: \"Complete first draft of 'The Last Ember'\" (draft)\n- Focus entity: The scene where Elena discovers the dragon forge went really well.\n\nIssues to address:\n\n- Need to strengthen the dialogue between Elena and Master Thorne\n- The pacing in the middle feels slow\n- Add more sensory details about the forge\n\nChapter 3 plans:\n\n- Elena's first attempt at magical forging\n- Introduce the Shadow King's herald\n- Foreshadow the prophecy\n\nAlso need to go back and fix continuity issue - Elena's age mentioned as 16 in chapter 1 but 17 in chapter 2.\n\n[5] role=assistant\nGreat progress on Chapter 2—congrats on hitting 4,500 words and nailing the dragon forge scene!\n\nI've captured everything in the project overview document and updated the task structure:\n\n**Updated:**\n\n- **Project Overview document** (a0487b08-21ef-45b4-9835-a5df42711f2e): Appended your progress update, issues list, continuity note, and Chapter 3 plans as a new section.\n\n**New tasks** (both linked to the \"Complete first draft\" goal):\n\n- **Revise Chapter 2: Strengthen dialogue, pacing, sensory details, and fix continuity** (e06da901-7d36-4b2e-8574-9e9d293ae8be)\n\n- **Write Chapter 3** (eee559f5-d097-41d8-aec0-70fddb8a5308)\n\n**Updated task:**\n\n- **Outline first three chapters** (53d862e1-c075-456e-9b5e-891f3daa6d1c): Added your Chapter 3 details to the description.\n\nTackle revisions next, start Chapter 3, or something else?\n\nI can help brainstorm fixes, research, or outlines.\n\n[6] role=user\nResearch notes for the magic system:\n\nFound interesting parallels with Japanese sword-making traditions - the idea of the smith's spirit entering the blade. Could adapt this: Elena's emotions during forging affect the weapon's properties.\n\n- Anger = fire damage\n- Sorrow = ice/frost\n- Joy = healing properties\n- Fear = defensive shields\n\nAlso researching:\n\n- Damascus steel patterns for visual descriptions\n- Celtic mythology about smith gods (Goibniu)\n- Types of medieval weapons beyond swords\n\nWorld-building additions:\n\n- The Forge Temples: ancient sites where dragon fire still burns\n- Smith's Guild hierarchy and traditions\n- The Quenching Ritual: how magical weapons are completed\n- Regional differences in forging techniques across Aethermoor\n",

        "content": "## Magic System Research\n\nFound interesting parallels with Japanese sword-making traditions - the idea of the smith's spirit entering the blade. Could adapt this: Elena's emotions during forging affect the weapon's properties.\n\n- **Anger** = fire damage\n- **Sorrow** = ice/frost\n- **Joy** = healing properties\n- **Fear** = defensive shields\n\n### Additional Research Areas\n- Damascus steel patterns for visual descriptions\n- Celtic mythology about smith gods (Goibniu)\n- Types of medieval weapons beyond swords\n\n## World-Building Additions\n- **The Forge Temples**: ancient sites where dragon fire still burns\n- **Smith's Guild** hierarchy and traditions\n- **The Quenching Ritual**: how magical weapons are completed\n- **Regional differences** in forging techniques across Aethermoor",

        "title": "The Last Ember - Project Overview",
        "content": "# The Last Ember\n\n## Main Plot\nA young blacksmith discovers she can forge magical weapons when the kingdom's last dragon dies and darkness threatens the realm.\n\n## Initial Needs\n- Develop main character backstory (orphan, raised by master blacksmith)\n- Create magic system based on metal and fire\n- Map out the kingdom of Aethermoor\n- Write character profiles for the antagonist (The Shadow King)\n- Outline first three chapters\n- Research medieval blacksmithing techniques\n- Design the prophecy that drives the plot",
        "type_key": "document.context.project"
      }

  {
    "id": "6182e559-e780-46e9-b564-6997c2f76029",
    "session_id": "13fc9ea8-13d7-4aee-8e2f-df1cce9bb524",
    "message_id": "4c21efd8-ef70-44dc-bb93-43effc02cde0",
    "turn_run_id": "f7ed85fa-9781-468a-b69f-a46994fcd133",
    "stream_run_id": "6716d74f-64b0-4872-bd28-40a8a6df008b",
    "client_turn_id": "cfb2bd4e-3cca-43c2-9acb-c76764c5a1cf",
    "tool_name": "create_onto_document",
    "tool_category": "ontology_action",
    "gateway_op": "onto.document.create",
    "help_path": null,
    "sequence_index": 1,
    "arguments": {
      "title": "Magic System and World-Building Research Notes",
      "parent": {
        "id": "a0487b08-21ef-45b4-9835-a5df42711f2e",
        "kind": "document",
        "is_primary": true
      },
      "content": "## Magic System Research\n\nFound interesting parallels with Japanese sword-making traditions - the idea of the smith's spirit entering the blade. Could adapt this: Elena's emotions during forging affect the weapon's properties.\n\n- **Anger** = fire damage\n- **Sorrow** = ice/frost\n- **Joy** = healing properties\n- **Fear** = defensive shields\n\n### Additional Research Areas\n- Damascus steel patterns for visual descriptions\n- Celtic mythology about smith gods (Goibniu)\n- Types of medieval weapons beyond swords\n\n## World-Building Additions\n- **The Forge Temples**: ancient sites where dragon fire still burns\n- **Smith's Guild** hierarchy and traditions\n- **The Quenching Ritual**: how magical weapons are completed\n- **Regional differences** in forging techniques across Aethermoor",
      "parents": [
        {
          "id": "a0487b08-21ef-45b4-9835-a5df42711f2e",
          "kind": "document",
          "is_primary": true
        }
      ],
      "type_key": "document.knowledge.research",
      "parent_id": "a0487b08-21ef-45b4-9835-a5df42711f2e",
      "project_id": "04af5167-e41f-4d10-931b-c257c12767bb",
      "description": "Research notes on the magic system (emotions affecting forged weapons, inspired by Japanese sword-making, Damascus steel, Celtic smith gods) and Aethermoor world-building (Forge Temples, Smith's Guild, Quenching Ritual, regional techniques)."
    },
    "result": {
      "message": "Created ontology document \"Magic System and World-Building Research Notes\"",
      "document": {
        "id": "c8c21fe2-db65-4ae7-9ae6-9646da3bc6d5",
        "props": {
          "body_markdown": "## Magic System Research\n\nFound interesting parallels with Japanese sword-making traditions - the idea of the smith's spirit entering the blade. Could adapt this: Elena's emotions during forging affect the weapon's properties.\n\n- **Anger** = fire damage\n- **Sorrow** = ice/frost\n- **Joy** = healing properties\n- **Fear** = defensive shields\n\n### Additional Research Areas\n- Damascus steel patterns for visual descriptions\n- Celtic mythology about smith gods (Goibniu)\n- Types of medieval weapons beyond swords\n\n## World-Building Additions\n- **The Forge Temples**: ancient sites where dragon fire still burns\n- **Smith's Guild** hierarchy and traditions\n- **The Quenching Ritual**: how magical weapons are completed\n- **Regional differences** in forging techniques across Aethermoor"
        },
        "title": "Magic System and World-Building Research Notes",
        "content": "## Magic System Research\n\nFound interesting parallels with Japanese sword-making traditions - the idea of the smith's spirit entering the blade. Could adapt this: Elena's emotions during forging affect the weapon's properties.\n\n- **Anger** = fire damage\n- **Sorrow** = ice/frost\n- **Joy** = healing properties\n- **Fear** = defensive shields\n\n### Additional Research Areas\n- Damascus steel patterns for visual descriptions\n- Celtic mythology about smith gods (Goibniu)\n- Types of medieval weapons beyond swords\n\n## World-Building Additions\n- **The Forge Temples**: ancient sites where dragon fire still burns\n- **Smith's Guild** hierarchy and traditions\n- **The Quenching Ritual**: how magical weapons are completed\n- **Regional differences** in forging techniques across Aethermoor",
        "children": {
          "children": []
        },
        "type_key": "document.knowledge.research",
        "state_key": "draft",
        "created_at": "2026-04-16T22:03:43.285979+00:00",
        "created_by": "85eba71b-56c9-4561-9dae-3828cf9922c4",
        "deleted_at": null,
        "project_id": "04af5167-e41f-4d10-931b-c257c12767bb",
